regressionCheck: No regressions. Attempt 2 changes were style-only (heading gerund→imperative, bold title added to note callout). All 15 factual claims remain unchanged from attempt 1.

claims.claim: The system returns results through `IContentQueryDataContainer` objects
claims.location: Section: Query result container properties, paragraph 1 (line 1026)
claims.evidence: IContentQueryExecutor.cs line 28: GetResult<TModel>(ContentItemQueryBuilder builder, Func<IContentQueryDataContainer, TModel> resultSelector, ...) — result selector receives IContentQueryDataContainer

claims.location: Section: Query result container properties, paragraph 1 (line 1026)

claims.claim: ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames gets the code names of member roles required to access the content item
claims.evidence: IContentQueryDataContainer.cs lines 52-53: XML doc 'Gets the member role names that are required to access the content item'. ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs line 48: calls GetMemberRoleCodeNames() confirming these are code names.
ContentQueryDataContainer.cs lines 36-38: RequiredMemberRoleNames getter returns (requiredMemberRoleNames ?? []).ToList().AsReadOnly() — null coalesces to empty collection
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Default interface implementation returns empty collection via GetValue

claims.evidence: IContentQueryDataContainer.cs line 60: property declared in the IContentQueryDataContainer interface with default implementation

claims.claim: For secured content items, the property returns the code names of all member roles required for access
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs lines 42-53: filters for ContentItemIsSecured, calls GetMemberRoleCodeNames(contentItemIds), then SetRoleNames on each container
claims.docFact: AREA-006: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor populates property as post-processing step

claims.claim: For items that are not secured, the property returns an empty collection
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs lines 36-40: early return if no items secured. SetRoleNames (line 66-68) only sets when roleNames.Any(). Default getter returns empty collection.
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Default interface implementation returns empty collection via GetValue

claims.claim: The system populates this property automatically through the `ContentItemRolesResultProcessor`
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs line 10: [assembly: RegisterImplementation(typeof(IContentQueryResultProcessor), typeof(ContentItemRolesResultProcessor), Priority = RegistrationPriority.Default)] — auto-registered
claims.docFact: AREA-006: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor populates property as post-processing step

claims.claim: `ContentItemRolesResultProcessor` runs as a post-processing step after query execution
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs line 17: implements IContentQueryResultProcessor. Line 32: PostProcessQueryResult(IEnumerable<IContentQueryDataContainer> containers)
claims.docFact: AREA-006: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor populates property as post-processing step

claims.claim: The processor works transparently — no additional code or configuration is required
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs line 10: assembly-level RegisterImplementation auto-registers at RegistrationPriority.Default. No developer opt-in needed.

claims.claim: `ContentItemRolesResultProcessor` uses lazy loading — member role data is only fetched from the database when at least one content item has `ContentItemIsSecured` set to true
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs lines 36-40: if (!allContainers.Any(c => c.ContentItemIsSecured)) { return; } — early return before any DB call
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Lazy loading — role data only fetched when ContentItemIsSecured=true for at least one result

claims.claim: If no items in the result are secured, no additional database queries are executed
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs lines 36-40: early return before GetMemberRoleCodeNames call when no items are secured
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Lazy loading — role data only fetched when ContentItemIsSecured=true for at least one result

claims.claim: The processor also resolves role names for linked items included through WithLinkedItems
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs line 34: GetAllItemsIncludingLinkedItems(containers). Lines 73-89: BFS traversal through LinkedItemsByField on each ContentQueryDataContainer
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Processor resolves role names for linked items (GetAllItemsIncludingLinkedItems)

claims.claim: To include secured items in query results, set `IncludeSecuredItems` to true in the query executor configuration
claims.evidence: ContentQueryExecutionOptions.cs lines 16-17: public bool IncludeSecuredItems { get; set; } = false;
